prompt: Create a project charter for migrating our legacy CRM to Salesforce. Team of 8, 9-month timeline, sponsor is VP of Sales.
assertions: produces a complete charter with all sections (Executive Summary, Business Case, Objectives, Scope, Milestones, Assumptions, Success Criteria, Governance, Risk Preview, Authorization)
business case includes strategic alignment to ≥1 organizational objective
contains 3-5 SMART objectives with KPI targets
scope section has ≥3 explicit exclusions with rationale
≥5 milestones with target dates defined
authorization block includes sponsor name (VP of Sales)
evidence tags present throughout — ≥70% [PLAN]/[STAKEHOLDER]
output file follows naming convention: 00_charter_{project}_{WIP}.md

prompt: Our startup wants a lean charter for a 3-sprint MVP. No formal sponsor identified yet.
assertions: flags absence of sponsor as a critical gap
either refuses to produce charter without sponsor or creates draft with prominent warning
uses lean format (1-2 pages) appropriate for agile/startup context
adapts objectives to iterative/hypothesis-driven format
does NOT produce a full 5-8 page PMBOK charter for a 3-sprint MVP

prompt: Write SMART objectives for a project to 'improve customer satisfaction through technology'.
assertions: rejects the vague statement as-is
asks clarifying questions or assumes specific metrics
produces objectives with specific KPI targets (e.g., NPS from X to Y)
each objective has a measurement method defined
each objective has a specific deadline (not 'ongoing')

prompt: We're rescuing a failed project that never had a charter. Create a recovery charter.
assertions: recognizes this as a recovery/turnaround scenario
includes root cause analysis of the original failure
references original scope if discoverable
includes corrective plan section not found in standard charters
sets realistic re-baselined objectives acknowledging prior failure
